asOf: Weekend digest — Sat, Jul 11 (Fri Jul 10 close)
headline: Risk-on into the weekend: SK Hynix's $26.5B US debut (7x oversubscribed) re-rates the AI memory complex, NVDA +4% leads semis bid, AAPL rips on renewed Broadcom deal — VIX crushed to 15.84 as institutional vol-selling dominates. CRCL +15% on OCC trust bank greenlight and DAL -4% on record fuel costs split the tape; S&P 500 +1.23% on the week, just shy of record.

sentiment.reason: Index green (S&P +1.23% week, ES flat pre-mkt) led by NVDA +4% and SK Hynix's record IPO; VIX crushed 13% to 15.84 = institutional vol-selling confirming risk-on, but CRCL/DAL dispersion and oil still bid on Hormuz tail keep it from being a clean melt-up.

movers.reason: OCC greenlights national trust bank to hold USDC reserves — stablecoin infra re-rate, mkt cap +$2.3B ahead of July 18 GENIUS Act.
AI chip bid resumes on SK Hynix US debut validation + AAPL/AVGO renewed chip deal; closes $210.96, +$8.18 from open.
Q2 record revenue $19.8B but fuel +77% YoY = highest in company history; EPS -26% YoY despite top-line beat.

news.headline: SK Hynix prices $26.5B US ADR offering at $149 — 7x oversubscribed, biggest foreign IPO in history
news.context: Direct US trading closes 21% P/E gap to MU; trades 'when-issued' as SKHYV today, switching to SKHY Monday. Validates HBM/memory scarcity thesis and re-rates the semis complex.

news.headline: Circle (CRCL) +15% on OCC approval to launch national trust bank for USDC reserves
news.context: Stablecoin infra re-rate; gives USDC a federally regulated home ahead of the July 18 GENIUS Act implementation deadline.

news.headline: VIX crushed 13.4% to 15.84 as institutions sell vol near zero
news.context: Largest single-session VIX drop in 6 weeks; institutions running short-vol into a CPI/earnings gauntlet next week — fragility rising even as tape looks calm.

news.headline: Apple renews Broadcom chip deal; AAPL bid resumes into iPhone 17 cycle
news.context: Multi-year AVGO chip commitment removes supply uncertainty for the iPhone 17 launch; pairs with SK Hynix validation to clear the AI-device overhang from last week.

news.headline: June CPI Tuesday, Fed Chair Warsh semi-annual, big-bank earnings kick off — full macro gauntlet
news.context: Triple-stack catalyst: CPI consensus +0.3% m/m core, Warsh's hawkish lean, JPM/WFC/Citi prints. Vol is being gifted into this calendar — fade the calm.

options.thesis: NQ green with VIX crushed to 15.84 — front-week IV mispriced vs realized into Tuesday's CPI + Warsh testimony. Dealers positioned short gamma, any hot print = violent squeeze.
options.signal: Buy QQQ weekly put spreads (29,700/29,400) into Monday close; VIX >18 = take profit. Defined-risk tail into the macro gauntlet.

options.thesis: VIX 15.8 with 10Y at 4.52% — vol is being sold at cycle lows into a packed catalyst calendar. COR3M still compressed at ~9.
options.signal: Long SPY 7,500/7,400 put spreads July 17, fund with short 7,700 calls — risk-reversal for the CPI tail.

options.thesis: Lagging the tape into Q2 deliveries; IV elevated (~55%) with no fresh catalyst. Straddle seller gets paid for waiting through the vol crush.
options.signal: Sell TSLA weekly straddle (~415) into Monday; vol gift, no edge in direction here.

options.thesis: +4% rip with IV still 50%+ post SK Hynix validation; dealer flow turning two-way as the AI-memory trade re-prices. Earnings-adjacent premium is rich.
options.signal: Sell NVDA Aug 220/240 call calendar against long stock — harvest IV into late-July print while keeping AI upside.

options.thesis: OCC trust bank is binary catalyst, IV expanding fast on $2.3B mkt cap add. 30D vol has 48-72h shelf life before GENIUS Act implementation on July 18.
options.signal: Sell CRCL weekly call spreads (95/110) into Friday close — IV crush setup, news-driven premium.

takeaway: Risk-on into the weekend close, but the VIX crush to 15.84 is the cleanest 'fragile' signal in weeks — institutions are selling vol at cycle lows into a triple-stack macro gauntlet (CPI Tue, Warsh, big-bank earnings). Trade: long NVDA into SK Hynix validation, fade CRCL IV into July 18 GENIUS Act, hedge with SPY put spreads. Don't trust the calm.

headline: Memory-trade rotation day: SK Hynix's $26.5B US debut at $149 (7x oversubscribed) re-rates MU -2.7% on the 21% P/E gap, NQ -0.4% drags while Dow/ES flat; CRCL +15% on OCC trust bank greenlight and DAL -4% on record fuel costs split the tape — Iran ceasefire holds but oil stays bid.

sentiment.reason: Index flat (ES -0.01%, NQ -0.43%, YM +0.24%) with single-name dispersion dominating: SK Hynix IPO re-rates semis complex, CRCL/DAL are binary catalysts, oil still bid on Hormuz tail — no clear risk-on/risk-off, just rotation.

movers.reason: OCC greenlights national trust bank to hold USDC reserves — stablecoin infrastructure re-rate, mkt cap +$2.3B.
Q2 record revenue $19.8B but fuel +77% YoY = highest in company history; EPS -26% YoY despite top-line beat.
SK Hynix ADRs priced at $149 (7x oversubscribed) re-rates memory complex; MU's 21% P/E premium to SK Hynix now in question.

news.headline: SK Hynix prices $26.5B US ADR offering at $149 — 7x oversubscribed, biggest foreign IPO in history
news.context: Direct US trading closes 21% P/E gap to MU; MU -2.7% pre-mkt as the new security trades 'when-issued' as SKHYV today, switching to SKHY Monday.

news.headline: Circle (CRCL) +15% on OCC approval to launch national trust bank for USDC reserves
news.context: Stablecoin infra re-rate; gives USDC a federally regulated home ahead of the July 18 GENIUS Act implementation deadline.

news.headline: Delta (DAL) -4% despite record $19.8B revenue — fuel costs +77% YoY, EPS -26%
news.context: Ed Bastian flags 'highest quarterly fuel expense in our history'; airline reaffirms FY $6.50-$7.50 outlook but fuel shock is the new operating reality.

news.headline: US-Iran tensions contained, WTI holds $72; Williams sees no permanent energy-price rise
news.context: Geopolitical tail live but not escalating; Brent +0.3% to $76.38, gold -0.4% to $4,103 as safe-haven premium bleeds. CPI Tuesday is the next real test.

news.headline: June CPI Tuesday, Fed Chair Warsh testifies, big-bank earnings kick off — full macro gauntlet
news.context: June CPI + Warsh semi-annual + JPM/WFC/Citi prints = triple-stack catalyst. Williams stays neutral; market pricing ~1 hike by year-end.

options.thesis: NQ -0.43% with VIX crushed to 16 — front-week IV mispriced vs realized into a CPI/Warsh triple-stack. Skew flat but tail risk is in the data, not the tape.
options.signal: Buy 0DTE QQQ 29,700/29,500 put spreads Tuesday if CPI hot; VIX >18 = take profit

options.thesis: VIX 16 with 10Y at 4.54% — vol is being gifted into a packed catalyst calendar. COR3M still sub-10, dealer gamma long above 7,560.
options.signal: Long SPY 7,500/7,400 put spreads July 17, fund with short 7,700 calls — risk-reversal for the CPI tail

options.thesis: Lagging the tape into Q2 deliveries; IV elevated (~55%) with no fresh catalyst. Straddle seller gets paid for waiting.
options.signal: Sell TSLA weekly straddle (~260) into Monday; vol gift, no edge in direction here

options.thesis: OCC trust bank is binary catalyst, IV expanding fast on $2.3B mkt cap add. 30D vol has 48-72h shelf life before GENIUS Act implementation on July 18.
options.signal: Sell CRCL weekly call spreads (80/90) into Friday close — IV crush setup, news-driven premium

options.thesis: -2.7% on SK Hynix P/E gap but the supply story is unchanged; 30D IV expanding 80%+, dealer flow two-way as memory complex repriced.
options.signal: Sell MU Aug 175/195 call calendar against long stock — harvest IV into mid-July while keeping AI memory upside

takeaway: The SK Hynix IPO is the cleanest 'rotation without panic' catalyst in weeks — semis complex re-rates, but the broader tape is flat and the calendar is the real risk. Trade: fade CRCL IV into GENIUS Act deadline, sell MU call calendars into the supply noise, hedge with SPY put spreads into Tuesday's CPI/Warsh triple-stack.

headline: Risk-off pre-mkt as Trump kills Iran ceasefire: oil +3.9% lifts crude, semis bleed again (MU -7.0%, NVDA extends slide), and SPY/NQ/YM all red 0.5-0.8% into Fed minutes.

sentiment.reason: Geopolitical shock (Iran ceasefire 'over') spikes WTI, reignites AI/semis unwind, and drags US equity futures lower into Fed minutes.

movers.reason: Samsung's 19x profit print signals memory supply glut; semis selloff accelerates.
Robotaxi/AI capex doubts + EV peer weakness; QQQ put OI builds at .
AI chip demand jitters persist after Samsung guide; tech ETF drag continues.

news.headline: Trump declares Iran ceasefire 'over'; fresh US airstrikes + tanker incident near Strait of Hormuz lift WTI ~4%.
news.context: Geopolitical risk premium returns; crude bid and risk-asset sell signal stagflation tilt into Fed minutes.

news.headline: Samsung Q2 profit jumps 19x but memory guidance underwhelms; MU -7%, WDC -6%, NVDA slides.
news.context: Confirms memory cycle peak fears; semis index (SOX) drops >4%, dragging Nasdaq to worst day in a month.

news.headline: Fed June minutes due 14:00 ET; market parses dot-plot dissent and inflation stickiness.
news.context: Rate-cut path in question as oil re-rises; 10Y +7bp to 4.55% signals hawkish repricing.

news.headline: BofA flags S&P 500 technicals pointing to a corrective Q3; SPY tests 50-DMA support.
news.context: Seasonal weakness + stretched AI valuations + geopolitics converge; hedge demand lifts VIX +3.6%.

news.headline: SpaceX falls below  NDX debut price; ARK trims AMD, adds SpaceX/X-Energy.
news.context: Private-mark-to-public handoff stumbles; rotation out of crowded AI names accelerates.

options.thesis: Heavy put OI at  with IV skew steepening; defined-risk put spreads for tactical downside.
options.signal: Bearish bias, elevated IV

options.thesis: VIX +3.6% and ES testing 7500; long VIX calls as hedge into Fed minutes.
options.signal: Vol expansion

options.thesis: Unusual call flow in 0DTE s vs. put selling; mean-reversion bounce candidate if semis stabilize.
options.signal: Contrarian long bias

options.thesis: Iran premium lifts crude; XLE calls benefit, -strike Aug calls seeing flow.
options.signal: Geopolitical long

options.thesis: Earnings-adjacent IV still rich; put calendar spreads to fade downside into late-July print.
options.signal: IV crush play

takeaway: Defensive rotation + energy bid into a geopolitical and Fed-minutes double-header. Tilt quality/healthcare, hedge tail with VIX calls, and respect semis breakdown until memory-cycle fears ease.

headline: Tech-heavy tape cracks pre-mkt on Asia chip unwind: NQ -1.0% as memory/semis (MU -5.6%, WDC -6.2%) sell off post-Samsung's record 19x profit, even as Dow bid, oil rebound on Hormuz tanker incident, and SpaceX's $4.3B-passive-inflow NDX debut keep the broader tape in two-way land.

sentiment.reason: NQ -1.04% on Asia-led chip complex unwind (Samsung -10% despite record profit, MU/WDC/SNDK -5% to -6%) plus hawkish Waller 'risks flipped' remarks pushing 10Y to 4.50% two-week high — only offsets are Dow bid, oil +1.1% on Hormuz tanker attack, and BTC holding $63-64K.

movers.reason: Memory complex selloff spreads from Samsung's 19x-profit miss-the-bill; NAND/HBM peer-derating, MU/SNDK all -5%+ pre-mkt
22% off peak as Samsung's record print fails to validate AI memory scarcity; Korean-supply overhang + SGH's 'rate hike on table' commentary
Pre-mkt slip into Nasdaq-100 debut (largest IPO ever at $4.3B passive inflow day) — historical pattern of NDX additions fading post-inclusion

news.headline: Samsung posts 19x operating-profit jump, but shares drop 10% — AI valuation jitters spread
news.context: Sell-the-news reaction in Seoul (-6.7% KOSPI) is the cleanest read on the AI memory trade: record profits don't justify record multiples. Dragged MU/WDC/SNDK -5%+ pre-mkt.

news.headline: Waller: 'risks have completely flipped' to higher inflation — 10Y hits 4.50% two-week high
news.context: Hawkish Fedspeak re-prices July 29 odds to 25.5% hike vs 74.5% hold; SGH's Duy adds 'rate hike on the table'. FOMC minutes Wed now the binary catalyst.

news.headline: SpaceX (SPCX) officially joins Nasdaq-100 today — JPM models $4.3B passive inflows
news.context: Largest IPO in history enters NDX; Coindesk flags 'historical warning' — prior megacap additions (PLTR, MSTR) saw post-inclusion volatility, not fresh rallies.

news.headline: Tanker hit by projectile near Strait of Hormuz — WTI +1.1% to $69.32
news.context: Oil snaps two-day drop on single-event risk; offsets the broader risk-off tone. Brent still -1% but WTI/Murban rebound; geopol premium back in the barrel.

news.headline: BTC reclaims $64K as Strategy-selloff FUD fades, ETF flows turn constructive
news.context: Decoupling from risk-asset beta: BTC +1.1% even as NQ -1%. 200W MA held; structural stabilization signal per on-chain metrics. Pairs-trade candidate vs QQQ weakness.

options.thesis: NQ -1.04% with VIX 15.9 (-2% up) signals dealer gamma flipping negative into the chip-led wash-out; put premium still cheap vs realized
options.signal: Long QQQ weekly put spreads (29,500/29,300) into FOMC minutes Wed; roll into 29,400/29,200 if VIX breaks 18

options.thesis: ES -0.14% with software bid (MSFT/CRM/IBM green) creating dispersion vs semis — COR3M sub-10 favors single-name over index hedges
options.signal: Long SPY put calendar (Jul 11 / Jul 18) for FOMC binary; pair with short MU calls to fund duration

options.thesis: Lagging the BE/AI-power theme but Robotaxi narrative alive; vol still elevated into Q2 deliveries
options.signal: Sell TSLA weekly straddle (260) into Wed FOMC; vol gift, no directional edge with macro binary ahead

options.thesis: -6.2% pre-mkt on Samsung selloff contagion; 30D IV expanding 80%+, unusual put flow in front-week 65Ps
options.signal: Long WDC Aug 60/50 put spread (~$2.30) into memory-trade unwind; defined-risk, single-name beta to AI memory capex

options.thesis: NDX debut day with JPM modeling $4.3B passive inflows; -1.7% pre-mkt suggests positioning not chasing — IV elevated into close
options.signal: Sell SPCX 0DTE call spreads (165/170) into 4pm rebalance; premium-rich on the largest-passive-flow tape of the year

takeaway: Two-way tape: Asia chip unwind is the day's signal (Samsung -10% despite record profit is the cleanest 'AI valuation' tell in weeks), but Dow bid + oil + SpaceX NDX debut keep it from being a rout. Bias: fade NQ/QQQ into Wed FOMC minutes, pair-trade long BTC / short semis, watch for the 25.5%-hike-odds to bleed lower on cooler minutes.

asOf: Pre-market — Mon, Jul 6 (Asia overnight mixed; Euro mkt open)
headline: Futures bid into July payrolls week: ES/NQ +0.4-0.7% as chip names (TSM +2% on Citi PT) and Micron memory bid offset oil -1.5% on OPEC+ Aug hike; BTC tags $63K for 5th straight green session, gold pulls back to $3,350 on USD recovery.

sentiment.reason: Risk-on continuation: NQ leading +0.7% on semi rebound (TSM Citi PT, MU memory bid), BTC 5-day win streak through $63K, oil -1.5% on confirmed OPEC+ Aug output hike (growth-friendly) — only mild headwind is gold -0.4% and lingering 10Y stickiness near 4.49%.

movers.reason: Citi raises PT ~32% to $3,800 ahead of Q2; sees higher 2026 revenue guide on AI demand
Blowout quarter re-rates HBM/memory; analysts call recent semi pullback a buying opportunity
Q1 revenue/guidance miss on Middle East tensions; industrial weakness vs. soft-NFP rotation thesis

news.headline: US stock futures up as chip shares recover, oil prices decline
news.context: TSM/MU lead semis bounce after last week's 5%+ SOX drop — sentiment is the driver, not new data

news.headline: OPEC+ agrees to further raise output targets from August
news.context: WTI -1.5%; growth-friendly disinflation impulse, but adds headwind to E&P names (XLE leading sector decliner)

news.headline: Fed June meeting minutes on deck this week; jobs miss already in the price
news.context: Soft June NFP (57K) keeps September cut probability elevated; 10Y steady at 4.48% caps duration bid

news.headline: Bitcoin outperforms gold: 5 straight green sessions through $63K
news.context: BTC +1.6% as risk-on bid and post-NFP weak-USD flows extend; key watch is ETF inflows today

news.headline: Spot gold pulls back as US dollar recovers from weekly loss
news.context: GC -0.45% to $3,350; post-NFP USD bounce caps gold but the trend higher on real-yield decline stays intact

options.thesis: Mega-cap bid off lows after Thu's -1.7% close; 7-day put/call skew normalizing
options.signal: Watch 705P / 720C weekly (Jul 11) — call wall building at 725 into FOMC minutes

options.thesis: Vol crush continues (VIX -2.5% to 15.8) as dealer gamma stays long
options.signal: 735C (0DTE) favored on gap-fill open; IV rank at 22 — fade premium

options.thesis: Reclaiming 280 support after Thu's -7.5% flush; option vol elevated ahead of Q2 deliveries
options.signal: 280/300 call spread (Aug) for mean-reversion, IV rank ~58

options.thesis: Oil -1.5% on OPEC+ Aug hike puts XLE at session low — defensive energy bid fading
options.signal: Bear put spread 90/85 (Aug) on relative weakness vs. XOP, IV rank 41

options.thesis: Blowout Q + semi rebound puts MU in front of memory HBM flow
options.signal: 160C (Jul 18) tactical momentum play; unusual call volume flagged premarket

takeaway: Risk-on continuation into a Fed-minutes-and-payrolls-loaded week: semis lead (TSM, MU), BTC breaks $63K for the 5th green day, and OPEC+ is the only obvious headwind. Bias long NQ/QQQ into 705 support; fade XLE on the oil drop.

asOf: Weekend — Sun, Jul 5 (Thu Jul 2 last close; Fri Jul 4 holiday)
headline: Holiday-shortened week closes mixed: Dow at record 52,900 on soft-NFP re-rating, but semis -5.4% and Marvell -10% drag Nasdaq; gold bid resumes and BTC tags $62K as dollar weakens into Monday reopen.

sentiment.reason: Cyclical/defensive rotation (XLV +2.6%, XLU +2.2% lead) overwhelms Dow-record but XLK -2.7% and Philadelphia Semi -5.4% keep the AI tape under pressure; soft June NFP (57K) is dovish for the dollar but the rotation, not the index, is the story.

movers.reason: AI data-center capex fears — 76% of rev tied to hyperscalers; $23B market cap erased in one session
Profit-taking after record; Q2 deliveries 480K beat estimates but FY outlook still in focus
Five-iPhone launch plan leaked; Mag-7 rotation bid into the holiday close

news.headline: Dow hits record 52,900 as June NFP prints 57K — softness re-prices Fed
news.context: April/May revisions negative; unemployment drops to 4.2%. Soft-landing-cum-cuts trade lifts cyclicals and bonds, dollar weakens. September cut odds jump to ~65% per FedWatch.

news.headline: Philadelphia Semi Index -5.4%, Marvell -9.8% lead AI capex unwind
news.context: MRVL exposed 76% to data-center; AVGO/NVDA/AMD all red. AI infrastructure narrative hits a real air-pocket — biggest single-day sector loss in 6 weeks.

news.headline: BTC tags $62K as whale accumulation forces $130M in short liquidations
news.context: Decoupling from risk-asset beta into Monday reopen; ETF outflows ignore price action. On-chain demand picking up; 200W MA held cleanly.

news.headline: Apple +4.8% on five-iPhone launch plan leak; Mag-7 rotation into holiday
news.context: TSLA profit-taking (-7.5%) and NVDA weakness hand AAPL the leadership baton for one session. Watch whether it holds into Monday open.

news.headline: Supreme Court rules Fed can't be fired without cause — markets welcome limits
news.context: Independence ruling caps the White House reach into monetary policy. Real-yield curve stays bid; supports the soft-landing re-rating already underway.

options.thesis: VIX crushed to 16.2 with NQ -0.78% — IV-RV gap tight, dealer gamma long above 25,800 into Monday reopen.
options.signal: Sell QQQ weekly put spreads (25,700/25,500); long gamma into the soft-NFP re-rate

options.thesis: Soft-NFP re-pricing = ES pinned near 7,500; COR3M still compressed at ~9 with July FOMC minutes the next catalyst.
options.signal: Long SPY 7,500/7,600 call calendar July 11/18, fund with short 7,400 puts — risk-reversal for dovish tail

options.thesis: Stock -7.5% post Q2 delivery beat with IV still ~58% — call premium expensive on the 470/500 strikes after the FSD rip.
options.signal: Sell TSLA weekly call spreads (490/510) into Monday open; vol gift, no edge in direction

options.thesis: +4.8% rip into holiday on five-iPhone plan, but skew still steep — calls expensive relative to put hedge cost.
options.signal: Long AAPL Aug 230/250 bull call spread; cheap convexity into Q3 print, IV at 28%

options.thesis: -9.8% on AI capex fears; IV expanding fast, dealer flow turning two-way post-print.
options.signal: Buy MRVL Oct 230/280 call spread as recovery hedge — single-name volatility selling opportunity

takeaway: Long-weekend tape is mixed-to-bullish: Dow record + soft NFP + gold bid + BTC decoupling is the dovish-re-rate trade doing its work, but the semi/MRVL unwind is the real signal. Index is the wrong trade — be long defensives (XLV/XLP/XLU), avoid semis, let TSLA mean-revert, and watch Wednesday FOMC minutes for the cut-odds catalyst.

asOf: Weekend — Sat, Jul 4 (US cash markets closed; July 4 holiday observed Fri Jul 3)
headline: Long-weekend tape risk-on: NFP miss + gold reclaims $4,100 + BTC holds $72K after dovish re-pricing — but oil under $68 and AAPL -2% into Monday's reopen keep the undercurrent cautious.

sentiment.reason: Dovish June NFP (98K vs 113K), downward May revision, and softer Warsh rhetoric push September cut odds to ~75%; equity index direction is up into the holiday close, with gold leading and oil the lone laggard.

movers.reason: Mag-7 rotation continues into holiday — AI-device pricing overhang lingers; tests $208 support
Off session lows, Robotaxi narrative quiet; FSD v13 fleet update keeps the bid propped
Dovish NFP re-rates AI capex; TSMC CoWoS confirmation + HBM3E ramp keep the memory/compute split-tape alive

news.headline: June NFP prints 98K vs 113K est, May revised down 12K — dovish surprise locked in
news.context: September cut odds jump to ~75% per FedWatch; curve bull-steepens, 10Y drops to 4.26%. Soft-landing-cum-cuts trade is the cleanest in weeks.

news.headline: Gold reclaims $4,100 (+2.5%) — strongest single-session bid in 3 weeks
news.context: DXY down 0.6%, real yields ease; haven demand returns as dollar slides on NFP miss. Test of prior all-time-high zone next week.

news.headline: Brent sub-$70 for a 3rd week; WTI $68.20 — OPEC+ unwind fully priced
news.context: Energy tape under pressure into the holiday; XLE -1.95%. Soft-landing narrative helps consumer/transports more than it hurts drillers.

news.headline: BTC $72,340 (+3.3%) — decoupling from risk-asset beta, ETF inflows resume
news.context: Crypto bid persists despite thin holiday tape; $60K now solid support. Cointelegraph flags potential bottom confirmation; cleanest read heading into Monday.

news.headline: TSMC confirms CoWoS capacity ramp — Q4 utilization 95%+, NVDA/AAPL lead orders
news.context: AI-supply thesis intact on the build-out side; semis split-tape (memory winners vs device questions) persists into July prints.

options.thesis: VIX crushed to 14.6 — front-week IV mispriced vs realized; dealer gamma long above 30,000 into the reopen.
options.signal: Buy QQQ weekly calls (30,100/30,300) Monday open; 0DTE IV too cheap to sell into NFP relief

options.thesis: Bullish NFP re-pricing + July 4 liquidity gap = ES pinned near 7,560; COR3M still compressed at ~9.
options.signal: Long SPY 7,600 calls July 11, fund with short 7,400 puts — risk-reversal for the dovish tail

options.thesis: Stock +1.4% into holiday with IV ~55% — call premium expensive on 470/500 strikes after the FSD rip.
options.signal: Sell TSLA weekly call spreads (490/510) into the holiday vacuum; vol gift, no edge in direction here

options.thesis: Brent sub-$70 with Iran de-escalation + OPEC+ unwind = XLE is the wrong place to be risk-on.
options.signal: Long XLE Jan 2027 60 puts (~$1.80) as tail hedge into H2 supply-peace premium bleed

options.thesis: AI-cloud resell narrative still under-owned; front-month skew flattening into Q3 print.
options.signal: Bull call spread 640/680 Sep; cheap convexity vs single-long call, IV at 32%

takeaway: Long-weekend tape is risk-on — NFP miss + gold bid + BTC decoupling is the cleanest 'soft-landing-cum-cuts' setup in weeks. Trade: long duration/tech into Monday's reopen, fade oil/XLE, let TSLA mean-revert. Watch Tuesday's ISM Services for confirmation and Wednesday's FOMC minutes for the cut-odds catalyst.

asOf: Pre-market — Fri, Jul 3 (July 4 observed — cash markets CLOSED)
headline: US closed for July 4 — global tape risk-on after soft NFP: ES +0.31%, NQ +1.14%, gold recaptures $4,100, BTC holds $72K, oil rout deepens as Brent slips under $70.

sentiment.reason: Dovish NFP re-pricing (98K vs 113K) + softer Warsh rhetoric + gold re-bid above $4,100 = classic soft-landing-cum-cuts trade; only oil/XLE lag.

news.context: Soft jobs + downward revision = September cut odds jump to ~75% per FedWatch; curve bull-steepens, 10Y drops to 4.28%.

options.signal: Buy QQQ weekly calls (30,100/30,300) post-holiday open; 0DTE IV too cheap to sell into NFP relief

options.thesis: Bullish NFP re-pricing + July 4 liquidity gap = ES pinned near 7,550; COR3M still compressed.
options.signal: Long SPY 7,600 calls July 11, fund with short 7,400 puts — risk-reversal for the dovish tail

options.thesis: Stock -2.8% into holiday, IV 58% — call premium expensive on the 470/500 strikes after the FSD rip.
options.signal: Sell TSLA weekly call spreads (490/510) into the holiday vacuum; vol gift, no edge in direction here

options.thesis: Brent sub-$70 with Iran de-escalation + OPEC+ unwind = XLE is the wrong place to be risk-on.
options.signal: Long XLE Jan 2027 60 puts (~$1.80) as tail hedge into H2 supply-peace premium bleed

options.thesis: AI-cloud resell narrative still under-owned; front-month skew flattening into Q3 print.
options.signal: Bull call spread 640/680 Sep; cheap convexity vs single-long call, IV at 32%

takeaway: Risk-on into the holiday close — NFP miss + gold bid + BTC decoupling is the cleanest 'soft-landing-cum-cuts' tape in weeks. Trade is long duration/tech into July 11 reopen, fade oil/XLE, and let TSLA mean-revert. Watch Tuesday's ISM Services for confirmation.

headline: NFP day tape firms near the flatline as gold bid, oil soft, semis still bleeding

sentiment.reason: Futures flat-to-slightly-green ahead of June payrolls; semis cap a -5% rout while defensive bid in gold and bonds caps the downside.

movers.reason: FSD v13 rollout extends to older fleet; ARK adds, Q2 delivery tailwind
Bloomberg: Meta building cloud unit to resell surplus AI compute capacity
Tests $200 as Meta capacity-resell narrative hits AI-infrastructure multiples

news.headline: June nonfarm payrolls print due 8:30 ET; consensus ~150K, focus on wage growth and revisions
news.context: Soft print revives Fed-cut narrative; hot print re-prices 'higher for longer' — biggest single catalyst of the day.

news.headline: Meta plans cloud unit to resell spare AI compute capacity (Bloomberg)
news.context: Re-rates hyperscaler capacity economics; good for Meta, bad for pure-play AI infra names (NVDA, MU, SNDK).

news.headline: TSLA +15% as FSD v13 expands to older vehicles; Q2 delivery tracker turns hot
news.context: Musk unlocks ~4M-vehicle installed base; ARK adds — momentum extends into consumer-discretionary tape.

news.headline: Gold extends gains on softer ADP, lower oil; 10Y holds 4.48%
news.context: Defensive bid persists; curve steepens modestly as rate-cut odds drift higher into the print.

news.headline: WTI slides to $70 as Trump flags smooth US–Iran talks progress
news.context: Supply-peace premium bleeds out; energy lags, helps consumer/bond proxies but pressures XLE and oil-leveraged EM.

options.thesis: Put flow heavy into NFP but IV is compressed — fade the doomers if print isn't a stunner
options.signal: Buy 0DTE 480P / 478P spread into 9:45 ET; size small

options.thesis: 0DTE vol crush in full effect; gamma pin near 7500
options.signal: Iron condor 748/752 for 0DTE; skew still rich on puts

options.thesis: Unusual call volume 7/18 320C and 8/15 350C; implied move 12% vs realized 9%
options.signal: Sell call spreads into the FSD euphoria; premium rich

options.thesis: Skew steepening fast, 7/11 190P volume 3x OI
options.signal: Bear put spread 195/180 for 1-week hedge against AI-capex headline risk

options.thesis: Front-month call skew flattens on the AI-cloud story; upside gap risk into Q3
options.signal: Bull call spread 620/650 Sep; cheap convexity vs single-long call

takeaway: Asymmetry favors fade-the-fear if NFP prints in-line or soft. Tech is washed out enough that a clean number triggers a squeeze into the close. Hardest trade is META/NVDA divergence — let the dust settle, don't chase TSLA above 320.

headline: Futures red and gold slips under $4,000 as Iran rejects direct peace talks; ADP misses (98K vs 113K est) and Warsh's hawkish tilt keep the dollar bid into ISM/FOMC — but BE +20% on Brookfield's $25B AI power deal keeps the AI-power trade lit.

sentiment.reason: Geopolitical (Iran talks off) and macro (ADP miss, Warsh hawkish, gold under $4K) are all risk-off, but AI-power flow (BE +20% after-hours) and the fresh Q2-record backdrop keep single-name dispersion alive — index direction is down but the underlying tape is selective, not capitulating.

movers.reason: Brookfield expands AI power deal to $25B; fuel-cell/data-center narrative re-prices overnight
Q4 revenue doubles on drone demand; stock hits record highs, but guides $165–$177M Q1 (mixed)
Pre-market drag as Mag-7 continues post-Q2 rotation; price-hike overhang from Friday's AI memory unwind still in the tape

news.headline: Iran rules out direct peace talks with US — WTI slips to $69.40 four-month low
news.context: Hormuz traffic normalizing but the lack of diplomatic channel keeps the geopolitical tail live; oil and gold both fading — classic 'risk-off without panic' tape.

news.headline: ADP June +98K vs +113K est — labor cooling into ISM/FOMC doubleheader
news.context: Soft print gives the Fed cover to stay on hold, but with 10Y at 4.46% (up ~8bps), bond market is reading 'resilient enough' — Warsh's hawkish remarks add to the pressure.

news.headline: Gold under $4,000 — YTD low as Warsh tilts hawkish, dollar bid
news.context: Haven demand evaporating in real-yield squeeze; bears awaiting Warsh confirmation as the next catalyst. Watch $3,950 as critical support.

news.headline: BE +20% after-hours on Brookfield's $25B AI power expansion
news.context: Sole bright spot in the AI complex — fuel-cell/data-center power narrative re-rates; not broad semis strength, but the AI-power subtheme is alive and well.

news.headline: BTC under $58.7K as Strategy sell-off fears weigh
news.context: Crypto can't catch a bid despite risk-off in stocks; tested 200-week moving average overnight. Liquidity tailwind fading, onchain demand quiet.

options.thesis: NQ -0.43% pre-mkt with VIX creeping to 17.6 — front-week IV still cheap vs realized, but dealer gamma flipping negative below 30,200.
options.signal: Buy QQQ weekly put spreads (30,200/30,000) into ISM/Warsh doubleheader; VIX above 18 = take profit

options.thesis: ES -0.17% and 10Y bid to 4.46% = real-yield headwind for duration-sensitive growth; COR3M still compressed at ~10.
options.signal: Long SPY 7,500/7,400 put spread July 11, pair with long TLT call calendars to fund the trade

options.thesis: Robotaxi narrative quiet, stock lagging the BE-led AI-power trade — call premium cheap but no catalyst until Q3 deliveries.
options.signal: Sell TSLA weekly straddle (240) into Q3 — vol is being gifted, no edge in direction here

options.thesis: +20% rip on Brookfield $25B deal — 30D IV expanding fast, dealer flow turning two-way on size.
options.signal: Sell BE weekly call spreads (310/330) into Thursday — IV crush setup, single-deal story has 48-hour shelf life

options.thesis: Iran talks off but oil under $70 = XLE bid is fading fast; unusual put sweep on Aug 65s Tuesday.
options.signal: Long XLE Aug 65 puts (cost ~$1.20) into Iran headline risk; tail-hedge into the back half of summer

takeaway: Risk-off without panic. Iran headline + ADP miss + Warsh hawkish = 10Y to 4.46% and gold under $4K, but BE's $25B AI-power deal shows the smart money is still buying the AI infrastructure trade. Index is the wrong thing to trade here — be long BE/short oil, fade AAPL weakness, hedge with SPY puts into ISM/Warsh combo at 10am ET.

headline: Q2 ends on a risk-on high: Dow tags 52K as Iran de-escalates, TSLA rips 8% to record, semis bid on AI power demand — but QCOM, NKE warn on margins and BTC can’t hold $60K.

sentiment.reason: Index futures green across the board on Iran/Hormuz de-escalation plus a record-high TSLA-led tech bid, partially offset by soft QCOM/NKE prints and a struggling BTC.

movers.reason: Powers record-high tech rally; May EU registrations +100%, Robotaxi narrative back on
AI power demand revives dip-buying momentum; data-center fuel-cell angle
Q1 rev $10.6B (-2.2% YoY), weak Q2 guide despite AI data-center push

news.headline: US-Iran halt military action, Qatar talks Tuesday — Dow tags 52K pre-market
news.context: Strait of Hormuz risk premium evaporates; WTI slips under $70, gold fades, risk-on across futures.

news.headline: TSLA +8% to record, leads tech rally; BE +9% on AI power demand
news.context: Robotaxi narrative re-prices plus fuel-cell/data-center beta drags the AI complex higher into Q2 close.

news.headline: QCOM Q1 rev -2.2% YoY, soft Q2 guide despite AI data-center push
news.context: Mobile remains the drag; data-center chip diversification story takes another quarter to land — semis split-tape continues.

news.headline: NKE Q4 EPS $0.12 vs $0.14 est, JPM cuts PT to $47
news.context: Consumer-discretion warning shot into H2: pricing power still weak, FX headwinds persist; XLY soft into close.

news.headline: BTC drifts under $60K as Strategy flags $2B sell plan; dollar bid
news.context: Crypto lags the risk-on tape — first sub-$60K close since Q3'24, onchain demand quiet, liquidation tailwind fading.

options.thesis: VIX slipped to 18.4 with NQ green pre-mkt — front-week IV cheap vs realized, dealer gamma positive above 29,800.
options.signal: Buy QQQ weekly calls (29,950/30,100) into Q2 close, fade VIX above 19

options.thesis: Index vol cheap relative to single-name dispersion; COR3M still compressed at ~9.90.
options.signal: Long SPY 7,550 calls into July 4 holiday — gamma supportive, theta works for you

options.thesis: +8% rip on record high, but front-week IV still ~52% — chasing risk is real into Q2 close.
options.signal: Sell TSLA weekly call spreads (470/490) into earnings cycle; IV crush favors premium-sellers here

options.thesis: +9% rip, dealer flow turning two-way post Russell rebalance — IV crush setup.
options.signal: Sell BE weekly call spreads (290/310) against long stock; fade into mid-July

options.thesis: Hormuz de-escalation = oil bid gone, XLE laggard into Q2 close.
options.signal: Long XLE put calendar (Sep/Dec) into July jobs print; energy is the wrong place to be risk-on

takeaway: Q2 closes risk-on with a record-high TSLA masking real weakness underneath (QCOM, NKE, BTC). Don’t chase the gap — VIX at 18.4 says vol is cheap, but Q2-end rebalance flows and Friday’s NFP make this a fade-the-rip tape into the holiday.

headline: Futures bounce on US-Iran ceasefire hope; tech still fragile, vol elevated.

sentiment.reason: Geopolitical relief lifts ES/NQ pre-mkt, but QQQ-SPX IV spread at 4-yr high signals defensive tech hedges; rotation out of AI persists.

movers.reason: FDA clears Lumvoa for thyroid eye disease, first approved drug
Premarket pop on Russell 1000/2000 megacap inclusion
Lags Mag-7 premkt, lobbying for CXMT DRAM access amid AI fatigue

news.headline: US and Iran agree to halt military action, talks set for Tuesday in Qatar
news.context: De-escalation of Strait of Hormuz tensions lifts risk assets pre-mkt; Brent back above $72

news.headline: VIX slips to 18.4 but QQQ-SPX IV spread hits 4-year high at 11%
news.context: Traders still paying up to hedge tech despite calmer headline vol

news.headline: Tech funds see ~$20B outflows week to June 24, bond funds +$7.33B
news.context: Rotation continues out of AI winners into defensives/fixed income

news.headline: SpaceX (SPCX) set to join Nasdaq-100 July 7, JPM sees $4.3B passive inflows
news.context: New megacap addition reshapes index weightings

options.thesis: Tech hedge demand stays elevated despite futures bounce
options.signal: Skew favoring puts; QQQ-SPX IV spread at 4-yr high — sell-rich front-month QQQ puts

options.thesis: Index premium normalized post Iran de-escalation

options.thesis: Worst month in over a year; robotaxi fleet ~40 vehicles
options.signal: Bear put spread into July on failed $600 PT defense

options.thesis: FDA approval binary cleared, momentum chasing
options.signal: Front-week call volume spiking; IV crush risk into Tuesday

options.thesis: Mag-7 laggard, down 4.8% last week, still -2% premkt
options.signal: Put calendar to harvest elevated IV into earnings cycle

asOf: Weekend wrap — Sun, Jun 28 (Fri Jun 26 close)
headline: H1 closes on a split tape: Micron +15.7% wins the AI memory trade, Apple -6.6% loses the AI device bill; Nasdaq -4.5% on the week, BTC first sub-$60K close since Q3’24.

sentiment.reason: Friday’s session was a textbook dispersion tape: Micron ripped on AI memory scarcity while Apple sold the AI cost-curve story, and oil re-priced on Hormuz risk — index level barely moved but the underlying signal was risk-off into the long weekend.

movers.reason: FQ3 beat & raise ($41.46B rev, 84.6% GM) — memory scarcity story wins the day; bottleneck moves from compute to memory
MacBook/iPad price hike flags memory cost pressure hitting consumer hardware margins — worst day in over a year
AI complex contagion — Apple’s pricing move read as a tax on AI-end demand; dealers drag NVDA into the long weekend

news.headline: Micron +15.7% on $41.46B quarter, Apple -6.6% — AI cost curve splits the tape
news.context: Memory scarcity vs device cost pressure — bottleneck migrates from compute to memory. Picks-and-shovels wins, AI spenders lose into H2.

news.headline: Hormuz risk returns: Brent +2.1% to $75.26, WTI +2.2% to $71.92
news.context: Single-event risk in the Strait re-prices oil and reminds the tape that inflation is not gliding to 2%. Airlines and freight names under pressure Sunday evening.

news.headline: 10Y ends week at 4.38% after May Core PCE prints 3.4% YoY
news.context: Front-end futures still pricing 1 cut by year-end but 10Y refuses to rally — duration discipline matters into July CPI/Jobs.

news.headline: Russell reconstitution Friday adds SpaceX, ~62 names — $12.2T benchmarked
news.context: Closing-auction liquidity event complicates the real signal: index mechanics, not fresh conviction. Watch breadth Monday for clean read.

news.headline: VIX pushes to 19.6 as Asian reversal hits Friday morning
news.context: KOSPI tripped circuit breakers, SK Hynix -8%, Samsung -5% — global tech correction, not US-only. VIX9D vs VIX3M in mild backwardation.

options.thesis: VIX up into the long weekend with NQ -0.85% — front-week IV rich vs realized, dealer gamma flips negative below 25,200.
options.signal: Sell weekly QQQ put spreads (24,900/24,700), keep long VIX3M call calendars into July 11 CPI/Jobs combo

options.thesis: COR3M near cycle low at 9.90 — single-stock dispersion means index puts under-hedge concentrated AI risk.
options.signal: Long SPY put spread (7,300/7,200) + long single-stock puts on AAPL/NVDA into July 11 expiry, defined-risk basket

options.thesis: Skew flattening near 240 — call premium cheap relative to realized, Robotaxi narrative still priced into Aug.
options.signal: Sell ATM straddle Aug, keep OTM call upside (300C) funded by the sale; cheap convexity into earnings

options.thesis: IV crush setup after +15% rip — 30D IV ~92% vs HV ~65%, dealer flow turns two-way post-print.
options.signal: Sell MU weekly call spreads (160/175) against long stock, fade IV into mid-July as gamma normalizes

options.thesis: Unusual call sweep on XLE Jan 2027 110s — ~$8M notional Thursday/Friday; energy is the only green sector.
options.signal: Long XLE call calendar (Sep/Dec) to harvest time premium while keeping upside into Hormuz tail

takeaway: Index levels lie. The real tape is inside the AI complex — Micron won Friday, Apple lost it, and Asia paid the bill overnight. Dispersion is the only trade until COR3M normalizes. Watch Sunday’s futures open (6 PM ET) for whether the long-weekend gap holds.
